instruction:
What is the radius of convergence for the power series $\sum_{n=1}^{\infty} \frac{z^{n}} {a^{n}+\mathrm{i} b^{n}} ( \, a > 0 \,, b > 0 \, )$?

Options:
A. $$
R=a \cdot b
$$
B. $$
R=\operatorname* {g c d} \left\{\left. a \right., b \right\}
$$
C. $$
R=\frac{1}{a} + \frac{1}{b}
$$
D. $$
R=\frac{a \cdot b}{a + b}
$$
E. $$
R=\frac{a^n + b^n}{n}
$$
F. $$
R=\left|a - b \right|
$$
G. $$
R=\operatorname* {m i n} \left\{\left. a \right., b \right\}
$$
H. $$
R=\operatorname* {m a x} \left\{\left. a \right., b \right\}
$$
I. $$
R=\sqrt {a^{2} + b^{2}}
$$
J. $$
R=\frac{a + b}{2}
$$
